reasoning: A major federal reconciliation bill, the "One Big Beautiful Bill Act" (H.R. 1), was signed into law in July 2025. This act made sweeping changes to renewable energy tax credits, including the clean electricity Investment Tax Credit (Section 48E). Under the new law, strict prohibitions against sourcing from "Foreign Entities of Concern" (FEOC) and "Prohibited Foreign Entities" (PFE) were made mandatory to qualify for the base 48E credit. However, the domestic content requirements—which dictate a specific percentage of U.S.-sourced manufactured products—were explicitly retained as a "bonus" or "adder" (providing 2% or 10% in additional credits), rather than being made mandatory for the base credit itself. The legislature merely updated the escalating percentages for this bonus to align 48E with 45Y. Because the primary legislative vehicle for 2025 has already passed and specifically maintained domestic content as an optional bonus while addressing supply chain requirements through the separate FEOC/PFE mechanisms, it is highly unlikely another bill will be enacted before 2026 to make the domestic content requirements mandatory.
